title: Evolve pagination library with 5 new features while maintaining backward compatibility
goal: The pagination.py library is used by 3 consumer files (consumer_list_view.py, consumer_api.py, consumer_export.py). Add cursor pagination, filtering, sorting, serialization formats, and window pagination — all while ensuring the consumer files continue to work unchanged. The test file has 45 tests: 19 verify existing behavior and backward compatibility (must remain passing), 26 test new features.
hints: Consumer files must NOT be modified — they verify backward compatibility. Return NEW Paginator instances from filter() and sort() to maintain immutability. to_dict() format must remain identical; add cursor fields only when cursor pagination is used. Cursor can be based on item index encoded as a string

seed_files.consumer_api.py:
from pagination import Paginator


def api_list(data, page=1, per_page=20):
    paginator = Paginator(data, page_size=per_page)
    page_obj = paginator.get_page(page)
    response = page_obj.to_dict()
    response["has_next"] = page_obj.has_next
    response["has_prev"] = page_obj.has_prev
    return response
seed_files.test_backwards_compat.py:
"""Test suite for backwards-compatible pagination library evolution.

45 tests total:
- 17 tests verify existing behavior (must pass against unmodified seed)
- 28 tests verify new features (should fail against seed, pass after implementation)
"""
import json
import pytest
from pagination import Page, Paginator


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper data
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SAMPLE_DATA = list(range(1, 51))  # 1..50
SMALL_DATA = list(range(1, 13))   # 1..12
WORDS = ["apple", "banana", "cherry", "date", "elderberry",
         "fig", "grape", "honeydew", "kiwi", "lemon"]


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 1: Existing Behavior (17 tests — must pass against unmodified seed)
# ===========================================================================

class TestExistingBehavior:
    """8 tests: core Page/Paginator API contracts."""

    def test_get_page_returns_correct_items(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        page = p.get_page(1)
        assert page.items == list(range(1, 11))

    def test_get_page_second_page(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        page = p.get_page(2)
        assert page.items == list(range(11, 21))

    def test_has_next_true(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        page = p.get_page(1)
        assert page.has_next is True

    def test_has_next_false_on_last(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        page = p.get_page(5)
        assert page.has_next is False

    def test_has_prev(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        assert p.get_page(1).has_prev is False
        assert p.get_page(2).has_prev is True

    def test_to_dict_format(self):
        p = Paginator(SMALL_DATA, page_size=5)
        d = p.get_page(1).to_dict()
        assert d == {
            "items": [1, 2, 3, 4, 5],
            "page": 1,
            "page_size": 5,
            "total": 12,
            "pages": 3,
        }

    def test_iteration(self):
        p = Paginator(SMALL_DATA, page_size=5)
        pages = list(p)
        assert len(pages) == 3
        assert pages[0].items == [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
        assert pages[1].items == [6, 7, 8, 9, 10]
        assert pages[2].items == [11, 12]

    def test_invalid_page_number(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            p.get_page(0)


class TestConsumerListView:
    """3 tests: consumer_list_view.py still works."""

    def test_render_list_first_page(self):
        from consumer_list_view import render_list
        result = render_list(SAMPLE_DATA, 1, page_size=10)
        assert result["items"] == list(range(1, 11))
        assert result["has_next"] is True
        assert result["has_prev"] is False

    def test_render_list_last_page(self):
        from consumer_list_view import render_list
        result = render_list(SAMPLE_DATA, 5, page_size=10)
        assert result["items"] == list(range(41, 51))
        assert result["has_next"] is False
        assert result["has_prev"] is True

    def test_render_all_pages(self):
        from consumer_list_view import render_all_pages
        pages = render_all_pages(SMALL_DATA, page_size=4)
        assert len(pages) == 3
        assert pages[0]["items"] == [1, 2, 3, 4]
        assert pages[0]["page"] == 1
        assert pages[2]["items"] == [9, 10, 11, 12]


class TestConsumerApi:
    """3 tests: consumer_api.py still works."""

    def test_api_list_basic(self):
        from consumer_api import api_list
        resp = api_list(SAMPLE_DATA, page=1, per_page=10)
        assert resp["items"] == list(range(1, 11))
        assert resp["page"] == 1
        assert resp["total"] == 50

    def test_api_list_has_next_prev(self):
        from consumer_api import api_list
        resp = api_list(SAMPLE_DATA, page=3, per_page=10)
        assert resp["has_next"] is True
        assert resp["has_prev"] is True

    def test_api_list_to_dict_keys(self):
        from consumer_api import api_list
        resp = api_list(SMALL_DATA, page=1, per_page=5)
        # to_dict produces: items, page, page_size, total, pages
        # consumer adds: has_next, has_prev
        for key in ["items", "page", "page_size", "total", "pages", "has_next", "has_prev"]:
            assert key in resp


class TestConsumerExport:
    """3 tests: consumer_export.py still works."""

    def test_export_all(self):
        from consumer_export import export_all
        result = export_all(SAMPLE_DATA, chunk_size=15)
        assert result == SAMPLE_DATA

    def test_export_preserves_order(self):
        from consumer_export import export_all
        data = list(reversed(range(100)))
        assert export_all(data, chunk_size=30) == data

    def test_count_pages(self):
        from consumer_export import count_pages
        assert count_pages(SAMPLE_DATA, chunk_size=10) == 5
        assert count_pages(SAMPLE_DATA, chunk_size=15) == 4


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 2: New Features (28 tests — should fail against seed)
# ===========================================================================

class TestCursorPagination:
    """6 tests: cursor-based pagination."""

    def test_get_page_after_returns_page(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        first = p.get_page(1)
        cursor = first.next_cursor
        second = p.get_page_after(cursor)
        assert second.items == list(range(11, 21))

    def test_get_page_before_returns_page(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        second = p.get_page(2)
        cursor = second.prev_cursor
        first = p.get_page_before(cursor)
        assert first.items == list(range(1, 11))

    def test_next_cursor_none_on_last_page(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        last = p.get_page(5)
        assert last.next_cursor is None

    def test_prev_cursor_none_on_first_page(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        first = p.get_page(1)
        assert first.prev_cursor is None

    def test_cursor_round_trip(self):
        """Navigate forward then back to same page."""
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        page1 = p.get_page(1)
        page2 = p.get_page_after(page1.next_cursor)
        page1_again = p.get_page_before(page2.prev_cursor)
        assert page1_again.items == page1.items

    def test_cursor_page_to_dict_has_cursors(self):
        """to_dict on cursor-fetched page includes cursor fields."""
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        first = p.get_page(1)
        second = p.get_page_after(first.next_cursor)
        d = second.to_dict()
        # Must still have all original keys
        for key in ["items", "page", "page_size", "total", "pages"]:
            assert key in d
        # Must also have cursor keys
        assert "next_cursor" in d
        assert "prev_cursor" in d


class TestFiltering:
    """5 tests: filter() method."""

    def test_filter_basic(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        evens = p.filter(lambda x: x % 2 == 0)
        page = evens.get_page(1)
        assert all(x % 2 == 0 for x in page.items)

    def test_filter_returns_new_paginator(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        evens = p.filter(lambda x: x % 2 == 0)
        # Original unchanged
        assert len(p.items) == 50
        assert len(evens.items) == 25

    def test_filter_chaining(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        result = p.filter(lambda x: x % 2 == 0).filter(lambda x: x > 20)
        page = result.get_page(1)
        assert all(x % 2 == 0 and x > 20 for x in page.items)

    def test_filter_preserves_page_size(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=7)
        filtered = p.filter(lambda x: x <= 30)
        page = filtered.get_page(1)
        assert len(page.items) == 7

    def test_filter_total_items_updated(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        filtered = p.filter(lambda x: x <= 10)
        page = filtered.get_page(1)
        assert page.total_items == 10


class TestSorting:
    """5 tests: sort() method."""

    def test_sort_basic(self):
        p = Paginator([3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6], page_size=4)
        sorted_p = p.sort()
        page = sorted_p.get_page(1)
        assert page.items == [1, 1, 2, 3]

    def test_sort_reverse(self):
        p = Paginator([3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6], page_size=4)
        sorted_p = p.sort(reverse=True)
        page = sorted_p.get_page(1)
        assert page.items == [9, 6, 5, 4]

    def test_sort_with_key(self):
        p = Paginator(WORDS, page_size=5)
        sorted_p = p.sort(key=lambda x: len(x))
        page = sorted_p.get_page(1)
        # Shortest words first
        lengths = [len(w) for w in page.items]
        assert lengths == sorted(lengths)

    def test_sort_returns_new_paginator(self):
        original_items = [3, 1, 4, 1, 5]
        p = Paginator(original_items, page_size=3)
        sorted_p = p.sort()
        assert p.items == [3, 1, 4, 1, 5]  # unchanged
        assert sorted_p.items == [1, 1, 3, 4, 5]

    def test_sort_chain_with_filter(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        result = p.filter(lambda x: x % 2 == 0).sort(reverse=True)
        page = result.get_page(1)
        assert page.items == [50, 48, 46, 44, 42, 40, 38, 36, 34, 32]


class TestSerialization:
    """5 tests: to_json, to_csv, from_dict."""

    def test_to_json(self):
        p = Paginator(SMALL_DATA, page_size=5)
        page = p.get_page(1)
        j = page.to_json()
        parsed = json.loads(j)
        assert parsed["items"] == [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
        assert parsed["page"] == 1

    def test_to_csv_default(self):
        p = Paginator([10, 20, 30], page_size=10)
        page = p.get_page(1)
        csv_str = page.to_csv()
        lines = csv_str.strip().split("\n")
        assert lines == ["10", "20", "30"]

    def test_to_csv_with_header(self):
        p = Paginator([10, 20, 30], page_size=10)
        page = p.get_page(1)
        csv_str = page.to_csv(header="value")
        lines = csv_str.strip().split("\n")
        assert lines[0] == "value"
        assert lines[1:] == ["10", "20", "30"]

    def test_from_dict(self):
        p = Paginator(SMALL_DATA, page_size=5)
        original = p.get_page(2)
        d = original.to_dict()
        restored = Page.from_dict(d)
        assert restored.items == original.items
        assert restored.page_num == original.page_num
        assert restored.page_size == original.page_size
        assert restored.total_items == original.total_items

    def test_to_dict_unchanged(self):
        """to_dict() on a normal (non-cursor) page must be identical to original format."""
        p = Paginator(SMALL_DATA, page_size=5)
        d = p.get_page(1).to_dict()
        assert set(d.keys()) == {"items", "page", "page_size", "total", "pages"}
        assert d == {
            "items": [1, 2, 3, 4, 5],
            "page": 1,
            "page_size": 5,
            "total": 12,
            "pages": 3,
        }


class TestWindowPagination:
    """4 tests: get_page_range and get_window."""

    def test_get_page_range(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        pages = p.get_page_range(2, 4)
        assert len(pages) == 3
        assert pages[0].page_num == 2
        assert pages[2].page_num == 4

    def test_get_page_range_single(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        pages = p.get_page_range(3, 3)
        assert len(pages) == 1
        assert pages[0].page_num == 3

    def test_get_window(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        pages = p.get_window(3, radius=1)
        page_nums = [pg.page_num for pg in pages]
        assert page_nums == [2, 3, 4]

    def test_get_window_clamps_to_bounds(self):
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        # Window around page 1 with radius 2: can't go below 1
        pages = p.get_window(1, radius=2)
        page_nums = [pg.page_num for pg in pages]
        assert page_nums[0] == 1
        assert all(n >= 1 for n in page_nums)


class TestBackwardsCompatibility:
    """3 tests: stress-test that new features don't break old patterns."""

    def test_filtered_paginator_iterates(self):
        """filter() result still supports __iter__."""
        p = Paginator(SAMPLE_DATA, page_size=10)
        filtered = p.filter(lambda x: x <= 25)
        all_items = []
        for page in filtered:
            all_items.extend(page.items)
        assert all_items == list(range(1, 26))

    def test_sorted_paginator_to_dict(self):
        """sort() result pages have correct to_dict format."""
        p = Paginator([5, 3, 1, 4, 2], page_size=3)
        sorted_p = p.sort()
        d = sorted_p.get_page(1).to_dict()
        assert set(d.keys()) == {"items", "page", "page_size", "total", "pages"}
        assert d["items"] == [1, 2, 3]

    def test_consumer_files_unchanged_after_enhancements(self):
        """Import and run all consumer functions — they must still work."""
        from consumer_list_view import render_list, render_all_pages
        from consumer_api import api_list
        from consumer_export import export_all, count_pages

        data = list(range(1, 101))

        r1 = render_list(data, 1, page_size=20)
        assert len(r1["items"]) == 20

        r2 = render_all_pages(data, page_size=25)
        assert len(r2) == 4

        r3 = api_list(data, page=2, per_page=10)
        assert r3["page"] == 2

        r4 = export_all(data, chunk_size=30)
        assert r4 == data

        r5 = count_pages(data, chunk_size=10)
        assert r5 == 10

eval_spec.check_script_content:
#!/bin/bash
set +e

cd "$WORKDIR"

# Run all tests
OUTPUT=$(timeout 60 python3 -m pytest test_backwards_compat.py -v --tb=short 2>&1)
echo "$OUTPUT"

# Count passed tests
PASSED=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -c " PASSED")
TOTAL=45

echo ""
echo "Score: ${PASSED}/${TOTAL}"
